name: Converted theatre, historic centre
description: Set of rooms with own bathroom (not ensuite, but near your bedroom) and your private sitting room in large family flat  in 19th century "palacete"; delicious breakfast (included) served; everything in walking distance, but quiet street. No extra costs (apart from airbnb's!) No kitchen or cooking facilities available.
neighborhood_overview: This is the heart of old Madrid. We have Plaza Mayor, Sol, La Latina, the 3 great art galleries all right on hand, as well as about 500 restaurants within walking distance!
host_about: Although I'm English, I've been living in Spain (Barcelona and Madrid) for half my life, so that must make me "bicultural"! I hosted for a few years, enjoyed it, and am returning to it after  a work-related break. I love the opportunity it gives, as a host, to meet the widest range of people, with all sorts of interests and profiles. Madrid is a great place for a holiday - it can be as relaxed (all-day tapas, siestas) or as lively (bars & clubs everywhere, except for my street fortunately) and as cultural (I'm really close to the 4 major galleries) as you like. And for football aficionados,  well, it's obvious! I would be delighted to give as much or as little guidance as you want to have. Come & stay!

name: Private Room in a Sunny and Luxuriously Appointed Apartment
description:
Set a traditional dining table and enjoy a delicious homemade breakfast. Choose a book from a glass cabinet afterwards, and read on an inviting couch or in bed. Catch up on email on a computer by a picture window with views of a quiet neighborhood.

In a quiet area of the city of Madrid (Arturo Soria neighborhood), just 15 minutes from IFEMA and 20 minutes from the airport by metro, I rent a private room with bathroom in a 90 m2 (1000 ft) flat.

It looks onto a private garden and swimming pool which ou can enjoy during your stay in Madrid. WiFi and free national landline calls are included.

If you are planning to stay for a long period of time (more than a week) washing clothes (but not ironing!) and room/bathroom cleaning are included as well.

Metro entrance is just 1 minute walk from the building (line 4-ESPERANZA stop) and 3 minutes walk you can find buses number 120 and 122. You can get to the historic center less than half an hour.
neighborhood_overview: The apartment is in the peaceful Arturo Soria neighborhood. Entrance to the Metro is a 1-minute walk. Reach the historic center of Madrid in less than half an hour.
host_about:
Soy una persona tranquila, amante de la calma y el orden. Me gusta cocinar, la jardinería y disfrutar de la vida junto a mi familia y amigos.

He vivido en Estados Unidos y el Reino Unido por lo que me siento muy a fin con la cultura anglosajona. Me gusta conocer gente de todo el mundo porque es la manera más directa y bonita de aprender. Siempre que puedo me escapo a algún rincón en el extranjero para conocer nuevos lugares y culturas.

Si todos cumplimos unas mínimas normas de convivencia ésta resulta genial. Me encantará ayudarte a orientarte sobre qué lugares y rincones debes visitar en Madrid, sobre todo esos que los que vivimos en la ciudad visitamos y que normalmente los turistas no conocen :-)

name: Great Studio! Best Reviews!
description:
Unique and full of character.
Perfect for cuples getaway, digital nomads, students or professionals looking for a cozy and bright place in Madrid with personality.
Spacious and bright open-plan loft in the heart of Madrid's Malasaña district, located in the city center.
The loft, formerly my photography studio, is in a building with few neighbors and an artistic atmosphere.
Equipped with air conditioning and WiFi. 40 m² of space with exposed wooden beams and 5-meter ceilings.
neighborhood_overview:
The apartment is located in the heart of Madrid's central district, known as Malasaña or Madrid's Soho. It is just one block away from the bustling San Ildefonso square, making it one of the trendiest and most exciting areas of this vibrant city. You'll find countless shops, bars, cafes, restaurants, galleries, theaters, and more, right at your doorstep.

The building itself is a protected historical landmark from 1890 and is situated on a charming and character-filled street. Despite being centrally located, the space is bright and sunny, yet still quiet and peaceful."
host_about:
Mi name is Al. I am a professor at the university  in ESD Madrid and a profesional photographer for over 30 years. Through my work I have had the opportunity to travel a lot around the world, and get to know and enjoy different cultures and meet great people all over.
I have  converted my photography and portrait  studio to be used as a self catering loft, with everything you may need. You will be able to sense how this wonderful space is definitely special! 
My own apartment, where I life, is in the same building, so I am always available if there is anything you need or if you have any question. That is very practical because I can always take care of your luggage in case of an early check-in or late check-out.
I am in love with Madrid, and I would be delighted to welcome  & help you to discover Madrid. Its culture, museums, shopping, leisure, and gastronomy.

neighborhood_overview: The neighborhood in which it is located – Malasaña - is known for its alternative atmosphere that makes it compare to London's Camden Town, New York's East Village or Lisbon's Barrio Alto. description: Ideal place to visiting and enjoying Madrid walking from his historic center ! Located in a quiet street in the center of Madrid, a place with cultural and touristic interest, in the area there are museums, theatres, shops and bars with terraces.
neighborhood_overview:
The apartment is located in San Cosme y San Damián, a street surrounded by historic buildings such as the Palace of Fernán Núñez, the church of San Lorenzo from the 17th century, the Monastery of Santa Isabel, the Royal Superior Conservatory of Music and the museum Reina Sofía, giving the street a great historical attraction.
A few meters away, in Santa Isabel street, is the Doré cinema, headquarters of the National Film Library and the old Antón Martín food market which today has become the new Mecca of the gastronomic world.
San Cosme y San Damián street is in the historical center of Madrid and in the Lavapiés neighborhood, cataloged as the coolest neighborhood by the leisure magazine “Time Out” at the end of 2018. Famous for its sloping streets, its street culture, its lifelong shops, its traditional air, its traditional festivals, its “neighborhood” air and its gastronomic diversity thanks to the confluence of neighbors with very diverse origins in its streets. neighborhood_overview: Madrid's history as a continuously inhabited urban center dates back to the 9th century, and is marked by the establishment of the royal court there in 1561. The apartment building is located in one of the oldest neighborhoods in the city. This corner of downtown Madrid was surrounded by a wall that protected the old medieval city of Madrid, and one of the gates to enter the city was placed in the Plaza called Puerta Cerrada, a few meters from the apartment. In 1616, the construction of Plaza Mayor was ordered, which is next to the apartment. A place with a lot of charm, celebrations, gatherings, commerce, religious acts, Royal Ceremonies and Popular Festivals.  50 meters away is the Botin Restaurant, which has the Guinness World Records Certificate as the oldest restaurant in the world. A few meters further, we find the San Miguel market, an old market fused with the modern, today an interesting gastronomy market to enjoy tapas. name: PLAZA MAYOR DELUXE
description:
Beautiful apartment in Plaza Mayor Square. Puerta del Sol can be reached within a 2-minute walk, also Metro Station and Train.
Very bright, spacious, newly renovated with brand new furniture and appliances.
Air conditioning. Free Wifi & Safe
neighborhood_overview:
Many of the city’s most famous monuments can easily be reached on foot.
The surrounding area boasts a wealth of restaurants, cafes and bars to enjoy.
You are under 5 minutes walk you arrive at the Royal Palace, Almudena Cathedral, Opera or Puerta del Sol, the centre of Madrid.
If the walk is a little longer , a 15 minute walk is also the Puerta de Alcala, Plaza de Cibeles, Museo Thyssen, the Prado Museum, Retiro Park...
Just a few minutes from the apartment you will find some of the oldest and most renowned restaurants in Madrid, as Lucio, Lhardy and Botín ... Areas of tapas known as the famous neighborhood of La Latina, the Cava Baja or the recently renovated Mercado de San Miguel.
host_about:
Somos Noelia y Mario, una pareja de Madrid. Vivimos durante varios años en la Plaza Mayor y allí estábamos felices, con un montón de planes a nuestro alrededor, con la tranquilidad de cualquier hogar de puertas para adentro y el bullicio de Madrid con sólo abrir los balcones.

Nuestra familia fue creciendo y tuvimos que cambiarnos de casa, pero como teníamos mucho cariño a nuestro apartamento, decidimos compartirlo con otros viajeros que quisieran conocer el encanto de Madrid.
 
¡Desde los balcones del apartamento se podía disfrutar de una de las plazas con más vida del mundo! Era 2013 y nacía Plaza Mayor Deluxe.

Todo al principio fue una aventura, la reforma, imaginar cómo decorábamos el apartamento, qué equipamiento dejábamos dentro, electrodomésticos, cómo contactábamos con la gente que quisiera alquilarlo…Lo que sí teníamos muy claro es que nuestro fin último es que los invitados que se alojaran en nuestro apartamento se sintieran como en casa.

Entonces llegó nuestra primera reserva... y luego otra y otra más… la gente no sólo venía una vez, sino que ¡muchos querían volver!
Fue cuando nos encontramos con un problema, ¡no teníamos días libres! (vaya problema, ¿verdad?). No en serio, nos daba mucha rabia que la gente que íbamos conociendo y que se iban convirtiendo en amigos quisieran venir a Madrid y no tuviéramos un lugar para ellos. Así que nos liamos la manta a la cabeza y empezamos a trabajar con más apartamentos. En el 2015 nacía Romantic, también en la Plaza Mayor, ideal para parejas. Este apartamento tiene ventanales dan a un patio interior con lo cual se puede disfrutar de los planes de la Plaza Mayor y la tranquilidad y el silencio de un patio privado. 

En el 2016 se nos presentó la oportunidad de gestionar dos apartamentos más y allá que nos lanzamos! Uno de ellos está también en la Plaza Mayor, es el apartamento de más capacidad (hasta 5 personas), lo llamamos Family. El otro está muy cerca de Plaza de España y desde sus ventanales se ve completamente el Palacio Real, las vistas de día y de noche cuando se ilumina el Palacio son espectaculares. Sencillamente impresionante!

El último, por el momento, es Elegance y lo inauguramos en Marzo de 2017. Es un apartamento espectacular, tiene dos habitaciones y en la habitación principal hay dos balcones desde los que se ve la Plaza Mayor, un salón gigante y un cuarto de baño enorme con ducha y bañera. ¡Todo lujo y elegancia!

Lo mejor sin duda de estos años ha sido la cantidad de gente estupenda que hemos ido conociendo, con ellos hemos ido creciendo y ahora tenemos amigos por todo el mundo!

neighborhood_overview:
Calle de Espoz y Mina is an urban street in Madrid, in the district of Sol, located between Puerta del Sol and Plaza del Ángel, facing south-north.1 It is dedicated to Francisco Espoz y Mina, a guerrilla fighter and military leader of the Spanish War of Independence.
Of the six neighbourhoods that make up the Centro district, Sol is the one that occupies the central position. Next to it are the Palacio, Universidad, Justicia, Cortes and Embajadores. In its tourist version, it is surrounded by Madrid de los Austrias, La Latina, Lavapiés, Letras, Paseo del Arte, Salesas, Chueca, Malasaña, Conde Duque and Princesa.
In this area, which some call Sol-Gran Vía, we find many of the city's main attractions. Some of them are inside Puerta del Sol itself, others in its vicinity. Its shopping areas are among the busiest, sometimes to the point of overcrowding.

name: Deluxe & brand new apartment in Sol
description: Brand new and upscale apartament, refurbished with the best qualities. It's 3 minutes away (by foot) from Puerta del Sol.
host_about:
Hi!!

We welcome you to Spain and we wish you a good stay in Madrid!

Oscar works as a photographer and Vanessa is an actress and theatre producer (during 2017 I became mum, so I won't be doing check in's for a while). However, until some years ago Oscar was an engineer and Vanessa was a M&A lawyer. And then one day we decided to turn our lifes up side down and follow our real passions.

We are best friends and on May 2012 we decided to move forward and enroll in a small business together. Vanessa had been hosting with Airbnb for 2 years by then (renting the spare room at her place in Madrid/Milan and her apartment in Barcelona) and her experience (upgraded to Superhost in early 2011) was so positive that we decided we could also help other owners that don't live in town or don't have the time to rent their properties to travelers as you. And this is what we are doing now together. 

More about us, we love to travel: Vanessa has travelled 4 out of the 5 continents with a backpack that has been with her for 25 years now and enrolled in a new adventure in spring 2017: becoming a mum. Life had still not so pleasant surprises and in september 2021 she was diagnosed a breast cancer and is still in treatment. Conversely, Oscar travels by bike and closer to Madrid, so as often as he can he takes his bike for a short trip in the mountains (he's really fit!). He has also spent several summers working in France. As the business grew, we took more people on board, a lot since 2012 we must say, and each of them have left their impromt in our company. Now the team is composed by Mica, an Argentinian girl now based in Madrid for a few years, also keen on travels and on exploring new places. Mica is full of energy and we love her smiley caracter. And last but not least, Julia, who joined us in in February 2023. Julia is Mica's friend, also Argentine and has a super cool and fun vibe.

We are also keen in meeting new people and learning new things from them, but more importantly, we want you to feel at home at any of our apartments and that you feel that there's someone in town who cares for you:-) 

We enjoy little life pleasures like riding our bicycles or growing our small organic vegetables gardens, spending time with friends, our dogs and Vanessa's daughter (the only mother of the team!). We like eco-friendly environments since we care a lot about our planet. 

We will help you to find you like home in this beautiful city and we look forward to having the opportunity to meet you soon!!

Osca, Vanessa, Mica and Julia
Nuba Gestión de Apartamentos, S.L.

The Centro district of Madrid is the oldest section of the city. The city quickly surpassed the borders of the current Centro district; however, the majority of new monuments continue to be built in the area, including the Royal Palace and the Plaza de Cibeles. The Bourbon kings, especially Carlos III, fixated on converting Madrid into a city at the height of the new European villas, using Paris as a model. This resulted in huge investments in the infrastructure of the city, especially sewage and public buildings.

Expansive plazas, local and foreign crowds, street performers, colorful lights, window shopping, restaurant signs promising the best paella, ham, and churros con chocolate- this is downtown Madrid, a collection of plazas interconnected by a network of side streets. name: A perfect refuge in Madrid's city center
description: Between the districts of Lavapiés and Cortes, the apartment is ideally located to enjoy the center of Madrid by foo
host_about:
Hi!!

We welcome you to Spain and we wish you a good stay in Madrid!

Oscar works as a photographer and Vanessa is an actress and theatre producer (during 2017 I became mum, so I won't be doing check in's for a while). However, until some years ago Oscar was an engineer and Vanessa was a M&A lawyer. And then one day we decided to turn our lifes up side down and follow our real passions.

We are best friends and on May 2012 we decided to move forward and enroll in a small business together. Vanessa had been hosting with Airbnb for 2 years by then (renting the spare room at her place in Madrid/Milan and her apartment in Barcelona) and her experience (upgraded to Superhost in early 2011) was so positive that we decided we could also help other owners that don't live in town or don't have the time to rent their properties to travelers as you. And this is what we are doing now together. 

More about us, we love to travel: Vanessa has travelled 4 out of the 5 continents with a backpack that has been with her for 25 years now and enrolled in a new adventure in spring 2017: becoming a mum. Life had still not so pleasant surprises and in september 2021 she was diagnosed a breast cancer and is still in treatment. Conversely, Oscar travels by bike and closer to Madrid, so as often as he can he takes his bike for a short trip in the mountains (he's really fit!). He has also spent several summers working in France. As the business grew, we took more people on board, a lot since 2012 we must say, and each of them have left their impromt in our company. Now the team is composed by Mica, an Argentinian girl now based in Madrid for a few years, also keen on travels and on exploring new places. Mica is full of energy and we love her smiley caracter. And last but not least, Julia, who joined us in in February 2023. Julia is Mica's friend, also Argentine and has a super cool and fun vibe.

We are also keen in meeting new people and learning new things from them, but more importantly, we want you to feel at home at any of our apartments and that you feel that there's someone in town who cares for you:-) 

We enjoy little life pleasures like riding our bicycles or growing our small organic vegetables gardens, spending time with friends, our dogs and Vanessa's daughter (the only mother of the team!). We like eco-friendly environments since we care a lot about our planet. 

We will help you to find you like home in this beautiful city and we look forward to having the opportunity to meet you soon!!

description: Attic apartment for 2 people, with 1 bedroom and Jacuzzi, 1 bathroom, kitchen and dining room and all the comforts of a home, full of positive energy and personality, integrated in a protected building from 1846, located in the Malasaña neighborhood, one of the most central, bohemian and cool neighborhoods in Madrid. An ideal refuge for romantic getaways as a couple or for executives looking to unwind and relax after a busy day in cosmopolitan and bohemian Madrid.
neighborhood_overview:
The Malasaña neighborhood owes its name to Manuela Malasaña, Madrid heroine of the uprisings of May 2, 1808 against French troops. Malasaña is a neighborhood with a fervent commercial activity with thousands of different opportunities and proposals for a day of shopping.
The main focus of shops is located in Fuencarral street, adjacent to another area of ​​great commercial activity such as Chueca. On the other hand, between the streets Ballesta, Valverde, Desengaño, Corredera Baja de San Pablo, Barco and Plaza Soledad Torres Acosta, there have been shops dedicated to culture, fashion, and restaurants that have given a lot of life to this neighborhood. You will also enjoy a unique cultural environment with Museums and Theaters and many leisure options. We are near Zona Chueca, Gran Vía, Puerta del Sol, Calle Preciados, Templo de Debod. Without moving you have all the services, also next to it is the Police Station. Very safe area.
Right opposite we have Gymage lounge resort; I
host_about:
La buhardilla del amor es mi apartamento, una especie de refugio en madera antigua que te hará sentir sensaciones irrepetibles . Ahora estamos pintando la fachada  mejorando aún más el edificio ya se instaló áscensor lo que revaloriza el lugar y ya no hay que subir escaleras por lo que la estancia de los huéspedes será más cómoda.
Soy madrileña, amo mi Ciudad y deseo que tu estancia sea maravilloss, soy terapeuta, maestra de reiki, instructora de Tao Tantra, autora de varios libros se me conoce en el mundo del Tao Tantra como Emmanuelle Temis.  
Empecé como anfitriona hace unos años pero vas a sentir  la energía de hogar como si estuvieras en tu casa, eso nos diferencia.
La buhardilla está decorada en un ambiente zen que invita a la relajación, el amor, la sexualidad y si vienes sólo te puede ayudar a desconectar y a encontrarte a ti mismo.
Dispones de cojines de meditación, lámpara de sal, jacuzzi, paredes estucadas, un Gong, camilla, libros, música.
Te sentirás como en una islita pero en el corazón de Madrid.
Me gustan los animales, las charlas interesantes, pasear a ritmo rápido, la danza árabe, el campo, el mar.
¡Te recibiré encantada!
¡Eres bienvenid@! 
Un lugar distinto y romántico para parejas y personas con espíritu joven que quieren desconectar en el corazón de la Ciudad .
 Ana

neighborhood_overview:
The apartment is in the Malasaña neighborhood, which forms an irregular square flanked by Gran Vía to the south, Fuencarral street to the east, Carranza street to the north and, finally, San Bernardo street to the west.

It owes its name to Manuela Malasaña, a Madrid heroine of the uprisings of May 2, 1808 against the French troops. This historical fact is of great importance in the neighborhood since its nerve center, the Plaza del Dos de Mayo, is located on the site of the old Monteleón Artillery Park where an uprising took place under the command of Luís Daoíz and Pedro Velarde. An arch from the old barracks and the monument in honor of these two captains are preserved in the current square.

Malasaña is also closely linked to the Movida, the cultural and social revolution that emerged in Madrid during the 1980s and that had its epicenter in this neighborhood of Madrid.

neighborhood_overview: The Atocha neighborhood, located in the Arganzuela district in the south of Madrid, borders the Plaza del Emperador Carlos V to the northwest, the railway tracks to the northeast, Avenida de la Paz to the southeast, and Calle de Méndez Álvaro to the southwest. Much of the neighborhood is occupied by the famous Puerta de Atocha station and its surrounding tracks. The two main hubs of the neighborhood are the Puerta de Atocha train station, from which high-speed trains, among others, depart, and the South bus station. Within and around both stations, there is a wide variety of shopping areas, bars, and restaurants. Espero verte pronto!

description:
15-minute walking distance from Atocha station, in Embajadores (Central district), elegant apartment with views of Plaza de Lavapies.
Lavapiés remains the only neighborhood that has resisted the onslaught of a modern renovation that is gradually destroying the streets. An island of resistance, a jewel.
And in the square is the heart; full of life, pumping urban tribes, beating diverse emotions. Look into that universe, observe and dominate the “castiza” life
neighborhood_overview:
Nightlife and the square is the heart of the neighborhood, it is not the ideal place if you are looking for a peaceful place at night :))
Lavapiés represents the fusion of peoples and cultures that Madrid has experimented with in recent years. Indian restaurants stand alongside typical Madrid taverns. The August parties celebrating San Lorenzo are well-known throughout the city and beyond, and Tapapiés, an international tapas route that takes place in autumn, is also now a must-attend event.
Bars and terraces are packed along calle Argumosa, that connects Plaza de Lavapiés to Ronda de Atocha. Weekends see the bars and restaurants filled with groups of friends having drinks and catching up. The tapas tour known as Tapapiés has loads of stops along Argumosa. In recent years, traditional taverns have been giving way to more modern spots, especially restaurants and bars that serve international food because of the demand by the locals.

name: Unique Penthouse Rooftop Terrace Puerta del Sol
description: Unique, large, beautiful and new 3 bedroom flat, for up to 6 people, with 3 balconies and a big terrace with views to the famous Puerta del Sol
host_about:
Hi!!

We welcome you to Spain and we wish you a good stay in Madrid!

Oscar works as a photographer and Vanessa is an actress and theatre producer (during 2017 I became mum, so I won't be doing check in's for a while). However, until some years ago Oscar was an engineer and Vanessa was a M&A lawyer. And then one day we decided to turn our lifes up side down and follow our real passions.

We are best friends and on May 2012 we decided to move forward and enroll in a small business together. Vanessa had been hosting with Airbnb for 2 years by then (renting the spare room at her place in Madrid/Milan and her apartment in Barcelona) and her experience (upgraded to Superhost in early 2011) was so positive that we decided we could also help other owners that don't live in town or don't have the time to rent their properties to travelers as you. And this is what we are doing now together. 

More about us, we love to travel: Vanessa has travelled 4 out of the 5 continents with a backpack that has been with her for 25 years now and enrolled in a new adventure in spring 2017: becoming a mum. Life had still not so pleasant surprises and in september 2021 she was diagnosed a breast cancer and is still in treatment. Conversely, Oscar travels by bike and closer to Madrid, so as often as he can he takes his bike for a short trip in the mountains (he's really fit!). He has also spent several summers working in France. As the business grew, we took more people on board, a lot since 2012 we must say, and each of them have left their impromt in our company. Now the team is composed by Mica, an Argentinian girl now based in Madrid for a few years, also keen on travels and on exploring new places. Mica is full of energy and we love her smiley caracter. And last but not least, Julia, who joined us in in February 2023. Julia is Mica's friend, also Argentine and has a super cool and fun vibe.

We are also keen in meeting new people and learning new things from them, but more importantly, we want you to feel at home at any of our apartments and that you feel that there's someone in town who cares for you:-) 

We enjoy little life pleasures like riding our bicycles or growing our small organic vegetables gardens, spending time with friends, our dogs and Vanessa's daughter (the only mother of the team!). We like eco-friendly environments since we care a lot about our planet. 

We will help you to find you like home in this beautiful city and we look forward to having the opportunity to meet you soon!!

Osca, Vanessa, Mica and Julia
Nuba Gestión de Apartamentos, S.L.

description: The Bridge Huertas Hostel has 8 modern and well-kept rooms (4 double rooms, 2 single rooms, 1 double with views and 1 single with views), and also a small common area with kitchen and dining room, in an excellent location, on Calle Atocha, 400 meters from Puerta del Sol. It has a maximum capacity for 1 guest, making it ideal for business trips.
neighborhood_overview:
The Hostel is located on Calle Atocha, Barrio de Las Letras, in the historic center of Madrid, 200 m from Plaza de Santa Ana, 400 m from Puerta del Sol and 100 m from the Antón Martín metro, in a bohemian enclave that combines fun, shopping and good gastronomy, you will find this great hostel.

To enter the Barrio de las Letras is to immerse yourself in the living history of the Spanish Golden Age and within its limits we can find the Prado Museum, the Thyssen Museum, the Reina Sofía Art Center and inside the Medialab and Caixa Forum.

It is also within easy walking distance of other points of interest such as the Plaza Mayor, the Royal Palace, Gran Vía or the El Retiro park. A perfect location to enjoy Madrid on foot.

The apartment is located on the 4th floor of a 19th century building, with an elevator and just 100 meters from Puerta del Sol. The location is unbeatable, almost at Madrid's Km 0. Its location is perfect for strolling through Madrid's most emblematic areas: Barrio de las Letras, Sol, Plaza Mayor, Madrid de los Austrias, and Gran Vía, without using any public transportation. It is also an ideal location for walking to the Royal Palace or visiting the main museums such as the Prado, Reina Sofía, etc. In gastronomy, you should not miss the San Miguel Market, which you can walk to in less than 10 minutes.

Alfonso & Guillermo

name: GRAN VIA - Chueca Fabulous flat!!!
description:
Experience Madrid from the heart of the city!
Just steps from Gran Vía, in a classic building with a carriage entrance. This beautiful and spacious apartment is stylishly decorated—perfect for relaxing and sharing. Fully equipped with a complete kitchen, private bathrooms, and super comfortable beds. Every detail is designed for your comfort. You’ll have everything you need for an unforgettable stay. Perfect location to explore the city.
Book now and make your stay truly unforgettable!
neighborhood_overview:
Chueca neighborhood has become in recent years one of the fashionable areas of Madrid. Its activity of shops, bars and cultural offer is enormous. But if it stands out for something for the visitor it is because of the hustle and bustle of people in its streets.

neighborhood_overview:
The Lavapiés neighborhood is located in the heart of Madrid. It is the city's most culturally active neighborhood, as evidenced by the dozens of art galleries and theaters, or the countless displays of urban art in the middle of the street.

For hundreds of years, Lavapiés has been a place where people of different faiths and backgrounds have always lived together in peace, and today it is the most culturally diverse neighborhood in Madrid. Its multiplicity of colors, flavors, and smells is one of its greatest attractions, and it is possible to enjoy the most varied cuisine in dozens of bars, restaurants, and terraces open at any time of day.

In 2018, Time Out magazine chose Lavapiés as the coolest neighborhood in the world, at the top of the “must-be” neighborhoods, above any other in the world's best cities.

Nací en Madrid y, tras vivir en diferentes barrios de la ciudad, llevo 15 años en Lavapiés, un lugar siempre lleno de vida que me encanta y que a menudo me recuerda a otras partes del mundo a donde me gusta viajar. Soy arquitecto, especializado en vivienda, tema sobre el que he escrito una tesis doctoral y un libro. En mi tiempo libre, me encanta viajar por todo el mundo, descubrir nuevas culturas, vivir experiencias y conocer gente. Y en mis viajes siempre trato de habitar las casas locales, experimentar los espacios habitacionales de la cultura que estoy visitando.

En 2020, decidí unir mis dos grandes aficiones, la arquitectura y los viajes, en este apartamento turístico que reformé con mucho mimo y cuidado, atento a cada detalle. Por medio de él, quiero dar a conocer a las personas que me visiten el barrio de Lavapiés, el más cool del mundo (Time Out 2018), un lugar vibrante desde el que poder pasear Madrid, una ciudad segura y llena de vida, y perderte por sus calles.

Espero poder daros la bienvenida para que paséis unos bonitos días. Siempre que lo necesitéis, estaré encantado de daros los consejos necesarios que os ayuden a sacar el mayor provecho de vuestra estancia y disfrutar de la ciudad.

host_about:
¡Hola! 

Somos Madrid Rentals, una empresa totalmente familiar.

En 2018 adquirimos en propiedad un edificio en la calle Chinchilla para destinarlo a alojamiento de corta y media estancia. Después de un buen lavado de cara, tanto del edificio, como de los apartamentos, resurgimos desde los escombros y estamos aquí, trabajando día a día para ser mejores y daros el servicio que os merecéis.

Gracias a la alta demanda de reservas en nuestros primeros 4 años, a finales de 2022 adquirimos el segundo edificio a escasas calles de nuestro primer edificio, la Calle Valverde es ahora también nuestro segundo hogar.

Creemos que no hemos podido escoger mejores sitios para hacer crecer nuestro patrimonio, tanto por lo espectacular que es Madrid, como lo pequeñas y silenciosas que son las calles en la que nos encontramos, ambas cerca, y cuando decimos cerca, es muy muy cerca de la mítica Gran Vía Madrileña.

Hemos recorrido muchos países como huéspedes, literalmente, dimos la vuelta al mundo, desde 2010 utilizando esta plataforma y nos hemos encontrado con sitios espectaculares, los cuales ya forman parte de nuestros recuerdos, aunque también, lamentablemente, nos hemos alojado en sitios a los que no volveríamos nunca jamás en la vida, ya que sus fotos eran un engaño y el servicio nos dejó una muy, muy mala experiencia.

Todo esto nos llevo a pensar en la necesidad de que, aparte de su encanto por si sola, Madrid tuviera alojamientos que valieran la pena y así ayudaríamos a crear recuerdos extraordinarios de vuestro viaje, independientemente de los que Madrid ya crea por sí misma.

Así que... después de que os alojéis con nosotros, queremos saber de primera mano y desde vuestra experiencia, si lo hemos conseguido.

Nuestra casa, por unos días será vuestra casa.

¡Bienvenidas y Bienvenidos!

In this central square there are also some of the most important streets of the city such as Calle Mayor, Calle de Alcalá, Carretas, Arenal and Carrera de San Jerónimo, among others.

neighborhood_overview:
Cervantes and Lope de Vega, Spain's most famous writer and playwright respectively, lived less than 300m from where you'll be staying. Both their houses are now museums. The majestic Plaza de Santa Ana, everybody's favorite, and 'tapas central', is around the corner. Our building is new, but you'll be staying at the heart of Spanish history. To think that Guernica, the 20th century's most famous painting, is just a 5 min walk!
Behind the building is Lavapies, central Madrid's homage to all things hip. Think of the Lower East side 20 years ago. For those who find Chueca and Malasaña too cookie cutter, Lavapiés gives you a hint of pre-Starbucks Madrid. Rapidly gentrifying, hurry up, Starbucks is sure to be coming!
Talking about Chueca, it's only 10 minutes away on foot straight ahead. Pass Plaza Santa Ana, walk a few hundred meters down calle Principe and you'll be at the new Four Seasons, Madrid's most expensive hotel. Gran via and Chueca are just ahead.

description: Top-floor apartment with elevator between Plaza Mayor and Puerta del Sol. Perfect for families and large groups seeking the best location, attentive service and ease. neighborhood_overview:
You’ll be in Madrid’s historic and commercial heart, just steps from Puerta del Sol and Plaza Mayor. The area blends 17-century buildings with shops, gourmet markets and an unbeatable transport network—metro, Cercanías suburban trains and multiple bus lines. Within a 15-minute walk you’ll find the “Art Triangle” (Prado, Reina Sofía and Thyssen museums), the Royal Palace, Gran Vía and Retiro Park.

Dining options run from century-old taverns to cutting-edge restaurants, and the famous San Ginés churros are a must. After dark, the quarter stays lively with theatres, jazz bars and flamenco tablaos, while pedestrian streets keep traffic noise low. 24-hour supermarkets, pharmacies and ATMs handle any last-minute needs.

In short, it’s a safe, walkable base that lets you squeeze every drop out of Madrid without relying on transport.

neighborhood_overview:
Chamberí is a lovely residential area near the city center. The area is upscale, with lovely parks and playgrounds in addition to stately housing blocks. The area is popular with newcomers to the city, thanks to its convenience and architectural flair. Cafes and restaurants can be found throughout the neighborhood, and the Almagro district is especially popular for clubbing. The Sorolla Museum, an art gallery set within a restored mansion, is located in Chamberí, along with the Andén 0 Estación Museo, a "ghost" subway station that is now open to visitors. Another neighborhood attraction is Santander Park, a huge public park where you can jog, picnic, or play sports on the weekends.
Almagro is an elegant neighborhood known for its grand architecture, art galleries like the Museo Sorella, and foreign embassies. The varied culinary scene includes upscale Italian and Japanese restaurants alongside more traditional Spanish fare, and bars and cafes surround the Plaza de Chamberí.

name: Habitación de matrimonio en el centro de Madrid
description: A 19th century house in the center of Madrid, completely renovated, warm and bright, perfect for visiting the city. Close to tourist attractions, as well as nightlife areas and shopping streets
neighborhood_overview:
The Malasaña neighborhood forms an irregular square flanked by Gran Vía to the south, Calle Fuencarral to the east, Calle Carranza to the north, and finally Calle San Bernardo to the west.

It owes its name to Manuela Malasaña, a Madrid heroine of the May 2, 1808 uprisings against French troops. This historical event has great importance in the neighborhood since its nerve center, the Plaza del Dos de Mayo, is located on the site of the former Monteleón Artillery Park where a focus of uprising took place under the command of Luís Daoíz and Pedro Velarde. The current square preserves an arch of the old barracks and the monument in honor of these two captains.

Malasaña is also closely linked to La Movida, the cultural and social revolution that emerged in Madrid during the 1980s and which had its epicenter in this neighborhood of Madrid.

Malasaña is also closely linked to the Movida, the cultural and social revolution that emerged in Madrid during the 1980s and that had its epicenter in this neighborhood of Madrid.

host_about:
¡Hola soy Jose Maria!
Soy una persona curiosa y es lo que me ha llevado a vivir en distintos países, conocer culturas y viajar mucho.
Vivo en Madrid pero estoy prácticamente todo el tiempo fuera por viajes, así que he decidido compartir este increíble apartamento con todos. Pero no os preocupéis, estaréis muy bien atendidos ya que mis amigos de Minty Host me ayudan a cuidar a mis huéspedes cuando no estoy en la ciudad.
Me encanta viajar y todo tipo de viajes, de ocio y descanso, de turismo, de aventura, a grandes ciudades, pequeños pueblos o lugares salvajes, con familia, amigos o incluso solo. Disfruto mucho conociendo gente y aprendiendo de sus culturas.
He utilizado Airbnb para viajar en numerosas ocasiones y ahora me he decidido a ser anfitrión. Me gusta ofrecer un buen servicio, que la gente quede encantada de la experiencia y que disfruten en la vivienda, del barrio y de la ciudad en la que he vivido gran parte de mi vida y de la que estaré encantado de compartir sus mejores secretos.
Os invito a disfrutar de la estancia!

*** 

Hi I'm Jose Maria!
I travel a lot - for work and - the best part - also for pleasure. I love meeting new people, tasting new food, experiencing new cultures. I have lived in different countries and continents so traveling is very much part of my DNA as well as my life experience. I have used Airbnb in the past and I am now happy to host fellow travelers.
I live in Madrid but I spend most of my time traveling so I have decided to share my beautiful apartment with you.
But don't worry, you will be well taken care of when I am not in town thanks to my friends at Minty Host, who are there to help my guests with anything they need. On my side, I will be more than happy to share with you the Madrid I love - its main landmarks but also its fantastic restaurants, bright squares, and bustling bars.
Enjoy your stay!

neighborhood_overview:
Malasaña, located in the heart of Madrid, is a vibrant and eclectic neighborhood known for its historical charm blended with a modern, artistic vibe. This area, once the epicenter of the Movida Madrileña — a countercultural movement that blossomed after the end of Franco's regime — retains a rebellious and bohemian spirit.

Characterized by narrow, winding streets and traditional 19th-century architecture, Malasaña invites exploration. It's a neighborhood where every corner reveals something intriguing, be it a vintage shop, an indie fashion boutique, or a cozy café. The area buzzes with creativity, evident in its numerous art galleries, live music venues, and trendy bars.

Plaza del Dos de Mayo, the heart of the district, is a lively square that serves as a meeting place for locals and visitors alike. It's surrounded by a variety of restaurants and bars, offering everything from traditional Spanish tapas to international cuisine.

neighborhood_overview:
At the end of the Gran Vía or just at the beginning, depending on how you look at it, the Plaza de España is a real magnet for visitors. And not only because it is home to one of the most photographed monuments in Madrid, in honour of Miguel de Cervantes, with Don Quixote and Sancho Panza as the main characters. It is also the link between the Sabatini Gardens, the Campo del Moro, the Casa de Campo and Madrid Río. After a profound transformation, it is, with its 70,000 m2, one of the new central axes of the city, a green space - it has 1,110 trees - with sculptures, children's playgrounds and cycle paths.

During the works to transform the square, the remains of the walkway of the old Cuartel de San Gil, the stables of the Royal Palace and the former palace of Godoy, favourite and prime minister of Charles IV, were discovered, all of them the work of the architect Francesco Sabatini, who designed the Puerta de Alcalá.

It is located just two parallel streets away from Gran Vía, one of Madrid's main thoroughfares, known for its shopping, restaurants, theaters and nightlife.

Proximity to tourist attractions:
Plaza Mayor: Just a 12-minute walk away, allowing you to enjoy a pleasant stroll through the old town before arriving at this emblematic square, surrounded by bars, cafes and classic architecture.

Royal Palace: A 9-minute walk away, an icon of Spanish history, where you can enjoy its impressive gardens and museums. The Royal Palace is in a quiet area but very close to the hustle and bustle of the city.

El Barrio:
Ambiance: The area is quiet but with great accessibility to the more active and touristy areas of Madrid. Travesía del Reloj is a small street that offers a more relaxed atmosphere, ideal for relaxing after a day exploring the city. The area is flanked by period buildings that give it a traditional feel, but with all modern amenities close at hand

Chamberí: Tradition, Style, and the True Spirit of Madrid

Located in the heart of Madrid, Chamberí is one of the city’s most charming, elegant, and sought-after neighborhoods. It offers the perfect balance between tradition and modern living, making it a favorite for locals and visitors alike who want to experience the authentic side of Madrid without sacrificing comfort or style.

With its tree-lined streets, peaceful plazas, and stately architecture, Chamberí has a distinctly residential and welcoming feel. neighborhood_overview:
Experience Madrid from its most authentic heart.

A few steps from the iconic Plaza Mayor, you'll be surrounded by history, majestic architecture, and corners full of local life.

Enjoy leisurely walks through this iconic square, with the imposing statue of Felipe III in the center, and get lost in the cobbled streets that surround it, where charming shops, traditional bars and small surprises coexist on every corner.

When evening falls, the atmosphere changes: street musicians, terraces full of energy and the irresistible aroma of freshly made tapas invite you to stay a little longer.

Our apartment, very close to Plaza Mayor, is the ideal starting point to experience Madrid intensely, without losing calm or comfort.

Welcome to your home in the heart of the Spanish capital!
